memoryArtifacts.headline: Bacon's Castle in Surry, Virginia
memoryArtifacts.description: Arthur Allen is remembered for building Bacon's Castle. Bacon's Castle is a manor home built near Smithville, Virginia, in Surrey County. It was one of the first brick houses constructed in Virginia. …

zh.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen於大約1602年出生在英國武斯特斯爾德洛特維其。他於1626年12月18日與Alice Tucker在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1654年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利素利。他去世於1669年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利巴根也斯 卡素享年68歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen于大约1602年出生在英国武斯特斯爾德洛特維其。他于1626年12月18日与Alice Tucker在英国结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1654年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利素利。他去世于1669年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利巴根也斯 卡素享年68岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Arthur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin. King Arthur was a British king of the 5th or 6th century, about whom virtually no historical facts are known. He ruled in Britain after the collapse of the Roman Empire and before the coming of the Germanic tribes, and a vast body of legends grew up around him in the literatures of medieval Western Europe. His name is first found in the Latinized form Artorius; it is of obscure etymology. The spelling with -th- was popular among the gentry families of West Yorkshire in the late 1400s, even before Henry VII , who may have hoped to capitalize on the legend, gave the name to his son. It remained in regular use in some areas and its popularity exploded in the early 19th century, largely as a result of the fame of Arthur Wellesley ( 1769–1852 ), Duke of Wellington, the victor at the Battle of Waterloo and subsequently prime minister. Further influences were Tennyson's Idylls of the King ( 1859–85 ), and the widespread Victorian interest, especially among the Pre-Raphaelites, in things medieval in general and in Arthurian legend in particular.

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen was born about 1602, in Droitwich, Worcestershire, England. He married Alice Tucker on 18 December 1626, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He lived in Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America in 1654. He died in 1669, in Bacon's Castle, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 68, and was buried in Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America.

de.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen wurde um 1602 in Droitwich, Worcestershire, England geboren. Er hat am 18. Dezember 1626 in England Alice Tucker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1654 hat er in Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America gelebt. Er starb 1669 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Bacon's Castle, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America und wurde in Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen è nato il circa 1602 nel Droitwich, Worcestershire, England. Lui sposato Alice Tucker il 18 dicembre 1626, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Vissuto a Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America nel 1654. Morì nel 1669, nel Bacon's Castle, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.Surry, Virginia, British Colonial Americ: イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スリー
ja.lifeSummary: Arthur Allenは約1602年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアドロイトウィチで生まれた。1626年12月18日にイギリスでAlice Tuckerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1654年にはイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スリースリーに住んでいた。彼は1669年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スリーバコン'ス カストルで68歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Surry, Virginia, British Colonial Americ: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리
ko.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen(은)는 대략 1602년 영국 워스스터실르 드로이트위치에서 태어났다. 그는 1626년 12월 18일 영국에서 Alice Tucker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1654년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리 수리에서 살았다. 그는 1669년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리 바콘'스 카스틀에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen nasceu aproximadamente 1602, em Droitwich, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Alice Tucker em 18 de dezembro de 1626, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele viveu em Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America em 1654. Ele faleceu em 1669, em Bacon's Castle, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.Droitwich St Andrew with St Mary Witton,: дроитвич ст андрю вит ст мэри виттон, ворсестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Arthur Allen родился около 1602 г. в местности дроитвич, ворсестиршир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Tucker 18 декабря 1626 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он жил в местности сурри, сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1654 году. Он умер в 1669 г. в местности бакон'с кастл, сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.
